task_completion.description: Navigate to the Jenkins 'New Item' creation page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins UI is visible with the 'New Item' form or page open, showing a field to enter an item name.

task_completion.description: Enter the expected job name into the item name field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'HelloWorld-Build' is typed into the 'Enter an item name' input field.

task_completion.description: Select the 'Freestyle project' option for the new job.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Freestyle project' list item is clicked or highlighted in the Jenkins New Item menu.

task_completion.description: Add an 'Execute shell' build step in the job configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the job configuration page, under the 'Build' or 'Build Steps' section, an 'Execute shell' block is visible.

task_completion.description: Enter the expected shell command into the build step.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The command "echo 'Hello from Jenkins!'" is typed into the 'Command' text area of the 'Execute shell' build step.

task_completion.description: Save the job configuration and view the created job dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The project dashboard for 'Project HelloWorld-Build' is visible, indicating the job was successfully saved.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Jenkins web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser with the standard Jenkins UI, rather than a text editor, mock interface, or static HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI to create the job, not terminal API commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing the use of 'curl', 'jenkins-cli', or direct manipulation of Jenkins XML configuration files to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved the job configuration rather than just filling out the form.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final screenshots must show the saved project page or a success confirmation, not just the filled-out configuration form.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected Jenkins job name is exactly 'HelloWorld-Build'. The expected shell command to be executed in the build step is exactly "echo 'Hello from Jenkins!'".
